metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC3 so that the verification REQUIRES a positive sidecar artifact (drop or explicit log trace showing the 5-condition conjunction was evaluated), or add a prerequisite step that constructs/selects a plan review guaranteed to produce at least one completion-qualified finding for the re-fire.
findings.finding: AC3 contains an escape clause ('or a documented case shows none qualified') that allows the acceptance criterion to be satisfied WITHOUT the new live code path ever demonstrably firing. Per E6(f), when a flag is flipped ON (i.e., a cutover to a new path occurs), at least one AC must exercise that path end-to-end; a negative attestation ('nothing qualified') bypasses this requirement entirely and cannot be distinguished from the flag being misconfigured or never reached.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'A re-fired epic/story plan-review demonstrably runs the floor (a drop appears in the sidecar, or a documented case shows none qualified)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the escape clause with: 'A re-fired epic/story plan-review produces a sidecar artifact where either (a) `dropped[]` contains at least one entry with `drop_reason="completion"`, confirmed via `jq -e \'[.dropped[] | select(.drop_reason=="completion")] | length > 0\' <sidecar>.json`, OR (b) the run log contains a line confirming the floor was evaluated and zero findings met all 5 conditions (requires the OPTIONAL logger.info from the What section to be implemented as a prerequisite).'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or a pre-step in the existing ACs) to confirm that `sha256(src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_completion.md)[:12]` still matches the hash recorded in `docs/calibration/completion_floor.md` before setting `completion_floor_active = true`; if it does not match, re-run `scripts/calibrate_completion_floor.py` first.
findings.finding: The plan's own calibration document mandates re-running calibration if the prompt hash changes ('A later prompt revision changes this hash and RETRIGGERS calibration before the floor may be flipped on'), but no AC step verifies that the current `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_completion.md` still matches the calibrated hash (`sha256:1778b6349c3f…`) before the flag is flipped. The hash is only printed by `scripts/calibrate_completion_floor.py` — never asserted in tests or CI — so a prompt edit between calibration and flag-enable would activate an uncalibrated classifier silently.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four items) / docs/calibration/completion_floor.md line 12
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] Confirm `sha256sum src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_completion.md | cut -c1-12` equals `1778b6349c3f` (the calibrated hash in `docs/calibration/completion_floor.md`); if it differs, re-run `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM=1 python scripts/calibrate_completion_floor.py` and update the doc before flipping the flag.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete sidecar-inspection command to AC3, e.g.: `jq -e '[.dropped[] | select(.drop_reason=="completion")] | length > 0' <path/to/sidecar>.json` (or the equivalent log-grep if the OPTIONAL logger.info is implemented), so 'demonstrably' maps to a runnable, pass/fail check.
findings.finding: AC3 names no concrete proving command for reading the sidecar to confirm a drop (or a confirmed-absent evaluation), leaving 'demonstrably runs the floor' as a prose claim without a deterministic in-session check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3; also 'What' section describing sidecar persistence
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: 'Verified via: `jq -e \'[.dropped[] | select(.drop_reason=="completion")] | length\' <sidecar-path>.json` returning ≥ 1 (drop case) or a log line confirming floor evaluation with zero matches (no-drop case, requires OPTIONAL logging to be implemented first).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step in the What section describing the act of re-firing a named review (e.g., the last epic or story plan-review), and tighten AC3's escape hatch by specifying what 'documented case shows none qualified' means (e.g., show the sidecar `dropped[]` array is present and empty, or show a log/trace that the floor check executed).
findings.finding: AC3 verifies a deliverable ('re-fire a review and demonstrate the floor ran') that is not described as a step in the What or Scope sections, making it an orphan criterion; additionally, its 'or a documented case shows none qualified' escape clause leaves 'documented case' undefined, so the criterion can be satisfied without any observable evidence that the floor executed.
findings.suggested_fix: In What, add a bullet: 'Re-fire [named plan review] after enabling the flag; capture sidecar output showing either a `drop_reason="completion"` entry or an empty `dropped[]` with a note that no findings met all 5 conditions.' In AC3, replace 'or a documented case shows none qualified' with 'or the sidecar output from a re-fired review is attached showing the floor check executed with zero qualifying suppressions'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC #2 (and the resulting operator note) to cover BOTH sidecar fields — `dropped[drop_reason="completion"]` AND `coverage.completion_floored_finding_ids` — so the audit path is complete.
findings.finding: The operator-note AC (#2) specifies auditing via `dropped[]` / `drop_reason="completion"` but omits `coverage.completion_floored_finding_ids`, which the What section identifies as a second sidecar field that persists suppressed finding IDs. An operator reading only the doc would have an incomplete audit path.
findings.suggested_fix: In the operator note, add: 'To fully audit suppressions, check both (a) `sidecar.dropped` entries where `drop_reason=="completion"` (full completion answers) and (b) `sidecar.coverage.completion_floored_finding_ids` (the list of suppressed finding IDs). Both fields must be inspected together to reconstruct what was dropped and why.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm and resolve parent-containment: either (a) re-parent this leaf to an appropriate ticket whose scope explicitly includes 'activate completion_floor_active in the dogfood project + operator monitoring documentation', or (b) if 66ac is the intended parent, update 66ac's plan to add activation + monitoring as an explicit post-implementation deliverable (which will stale 66ac's plan-review attestation and require re-review), then re-review this leaf against the corrected parent.
findings.finding: The leaf's declared deliverables — activating `completion_floor_active=true` in `rebar.toml`, adding operator monitoring/audit documentation, and (optionally) adding a per-drop `logger.info` — all lie OUTSIDE the parent epic's (66ac) declared scope and ACs. The parent epic's scope covers only the floor's implementation (Pass-2 sub-call, Pass-3 logic, delivered-now predicate, sidecar recording, evidence-gate config, calibration gold-set, tests). The parent explicitly frames the flag flip as a post-epic operator action ('Inert until an operator flips it on after the calibration gold-set clears its must-never-suppress bar'), not as a story deliverable. None of the leaf's ACs map to any parent AC.
findings.location: Leaf plan: What / Scope / Acceptance Criteria sections; Parent epic 66ac: Scope (IN/OUT), ACs
findings.suggested_fix: Create a new standalone story (or attach to an 'operations/dogfooding' epic if one exists) that explicitly owns 'activate completion floor in production config + add operator monitoring documentation,' and make this leaf a child of that story. Epic 66ac's scope was exclusively the floor's implementation, not its activation — this leaf belongs in a follow-on operational story, not as a child of the implementation epic.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
